channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Sanders: Government should break up OpenAI
item.description: Sen. Bernie Sanders (I-Vt.) said he believes the government should break up OpenAI, underscoring his concerns about AI's potential impact on the job market and interpersonal relationships.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Apple may turn off key privacy tool in Europe
item.description: In a victory for surveillance capitalism, Apple may be forced to leave its users in Europe vulnerable to rapacious ad data collection in response to “intense lobbying” by politicians in the region.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Trump 'terribly advised' on crypto billionaire pardon: Key supporter
item.description: Venture capitalist Joe Lonsdale, a key supporter of President Trump, suggested Thursday that the president was “terribly advised” on his decision to pardon Binance founder Changpeng Zhao.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: In a California farming region, researchers are mapping rural heat to protect farmworkers
item.description: In some of the most agriculturally rich regions in the U.S., researchers from San Diego State University are working to understand how climate change is impacting heat in rural areas and the farmworkers who toil in them.
item.dc:creator: Dorany Pineda And Jae C. Hong, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: The iPhone 18 Pro could come with a big Starlink upgrade – and it sounds perfect for off-grid adventures
item.description: We're starting to hear a few rumors around what Apple may have planned for the next set of flagship iPhones.
item.dc:creator: David Nield, TechRadar

item.title: EU accuses Meta and TikTok of breaching transparency rules
item.description: The European Union says Meta and TikTok have breached transparency obligations under the Digital Services Act.
item.dc:creator: Sam Mcneil, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: CIOs, be ready for agentic AI
item.description: CIOs are staring down a paradox. Employees are moving faster than ever with agentic AI. They’re using tools like Copilot, ChatGPT and embedded SaaS agents to build automations and streamline workflows on their own.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Qué medidas pueden tomar los CIO para progresar en la era de la IA
item.description: Si le pregunta a cualquier director de TI sobre la IA, probablemente le dirá que es ideal para realizar tareas repetitivas y de bajo nivel.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Python has a friend in Rust
item.description: Python 3.14 is here, and it’s bursting with goodies! Plus, Rust is making it easier to redistribute Python apps, PDM is simplifying Python package management, and Java is challenging Python for developing AI applications.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Enterprises should not install OpenAI’s new Atlas browser, analysts warn
item.description: Companies that might be eyeing OpenAI’s new ChatGPT Atlas browser should not rush to use it because of potential security risks, analysts said this week. The browser was unveiled on Tuesday after it had been teased for months as a work in progress. It is currently available for MacOS only.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: AI browsers can be abused by malicious AI sidebar extensions: Report
item.description: AI browsers may be smart, but they’re not smart enough to block a common threat: Malicious extensions. That’s the conclusion of researchers at SquareX, who on Thursday released a report showing how attackers can exploit AI sidebars through compromised browser extensions.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: New AI browsers could usher in a web where agents do our bidding—eventually
item.description: Welcome to AI Decoded, Fast Company’s weekly newsletter that breaks down the most important news in the world of AI. I’m Mark Sullivan, a senior writer at Fast Company, covering emerging tech, AI, and tech policy.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: Intel posts profit even as it struggles to regain market share
item.description: Intel posted profits in the latest quarter as the one-time America technology icon struggles to regain market share.
item.dc:creator: Bernard Condon, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump pardons Binance founder Changpeng Zhao, high-profile cryptocurrency figure
item.description: President Donald Trump has pardoned Binance founder Changpeng Zhao, who created the world's largest cryptocurrency exchange and served prison time for failing to stop criminals from using the platform to move money connected to child sex abuse, drug trafficking and terrorism.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Rare dinosaur mummies help scientists recreate their prehistoric lives
item.description: Researchers have unearthed a spooky pair of dinosaur mummies that seem to have been preserved in an unexpected way.
item.dc:creator: Adithi Ramakrishnan, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Living in giant moon glass spheres could be our future
item.description: California company Skyeports creates self-healing glass spheres from Moon regolith that generate solar power and support plant growth for sustainable lunar living.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: PlayStation’s Icon Blue Special Edition DualSense blew our legacy-loving minds, and we wonder how quickly it'll disappear
item.description: PlayStation's new, limited-edition Icon Blue Special Edition has officially launched, and TechRadar got our hands on one. Here are our first impressions.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Krol, TechRadar

item.title: Think you can trust ChatGPT and Gemini to give you the news? Here's why you might want to think again
item.description: AI assistants routinely misrepresent news, raising concerns about misinformation and public trust in the digital age.
item.dc:creator: Eric Hal Schwartz, TechRadar

item.title: Elden Ring Tarnished Edition, FromSoftware's Nintendo Switch 2 exclusive version, has been delayed
item.description: FromSoftware has announced that it has delayed the Nintendo Switch 2 exclusive Elden Ring Tarnished Edition to 2026.
item.dc:creator: Demi Williams, TechRadar

item.title: Netflix shakes up Stranger Things season 5 finale release with simultaneous theater and streaming debut in world first
item.description: Netflix isn’t letting the Stranger Things season 5 finale just shout into the streaming void as it confirms the episode will also come to movie theaters.
item.dc:creator: Amelia Schwanke, TechRadar

item.title: Intel faces high earnings bar as stock soars and cash pours in
item.description: Investors will be looking beyond the bottom line in chipmaker Intel Corp.'s third-quarter earnings after a wave of investments from the White House, Nvidia Corp. and Softbank Group Corp. sent the stock soaring 86% in less than three months.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: How AI can produce detailed storm surge forecasts faster and save lives
item.description: Hurricanes are America’s most destructive natural hazards, causing more deaths and property damage than any other type of disaster. Since 1980, these powerful tropical storms have done more than US$1.5 trillion in damage and killed more than 7,000 people. The No.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation, Fast Company

item.title: Google’s Quantum chip claims 13,000x speed advantage over supercomputers
item.description: Google Quantum AI has demonstrated what it describes as a verifiable quantum advantage — running a new algorithm on quantum hardware that outperforms classical supercomputers by a factor of 13,000.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Google’s Quantum chip claims 13,000x speed advantage over supercomputers
item.description: Google Quantum AI has demonstrated what it describes as a verifiable quantum advantage — running a new algorithm on quantum hardware that outperforms classical supercomputers by a factor of 13,000.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: What Americans think about the environmental impact of AI, according to a new poll
item.description: A new poll finds that as the United States rapidly builds massive data centers for the development of artificial intelligence, many Americans are concerned about the environmental impact.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Mcdermott And Linley Sanders, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Using the SkiaSharp graphics library in .NET
item.description: The news that the .NET UI framework Uno Platform project would be upstreaming features and fixes into the core multiplatform .NET libraries makes a lot of sense. The open source project has been working on cross-device .
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Police agencies turn to virtual reality to improve split-second decision-making
item.description: Police departments across the U.S. and Canada are adopting virtual reality training to better prepare officers for high-pressure, real-world situations.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: American e-waste is causing a 'hidden tsunami' in Southeast Asia, report says
item.description: HANOI, Vietnam (AP) — Millions of tons of discarded electronics from the United States are being shipped overseas, much of it to developing countries in Southeast Asia unprepared to safely handle hazardous waste, according to a new report released Wednesday by an environmental watchdog.
item.dc:creator: ANIRUDDHA GHOSAL, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Agentic labor for the U.S. military: Turning paperwork into strategic advantage
item.description: Agentic AI can run a whole workflow with minimal human oversight. The U.S. military, with a workforce of 3 million people, could be a top beneficiary. The post Agentic labor for the U.S. military: Turning paperwork into strategic advantage first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Meta cutting 600 AI jobs even as it continues to hire more for its superintelligence lab
item.description: MENLO PARK, Calif. (AP) — Meta Platforms is cutting roughly 600 artificial intelligence jobs even as it continues to hire more workers for its superintelligence lab, the company confirmed on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Federal News Network’s Industry Exchange Cloud 2025
item.description: Unlock the future of cloud innovation The post Federal News Network’s Industry Exchange Cloud 2025 first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: Big Tech is paying millions to train teachers on AI, in a push to bring chatbots into classrooms
item.description: SAN ANTONIO (AP) — On a scorching hot Saturday in San Antonio, dozens of teachers traded a day off for a glimpse of the future. The topic of the day’s workshop: enhancing instruction with artificial intelligence.
item.dc:creator: JOCELYN GECKER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Teens face new PG-13 limits on Instagram
item.description: New Instagram parental controls allow families to manage teen screen time and content limits through the Family Center with stricter safety settings.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US court tells Israeli company to stop targeting WhatsApp
item.description: WASHINGTON, Oct 18 (Reuters) - A U.S. court has ordered Israel's NSO Group to stop targeting Meta Platforms WhatsApp messaging service, a development the spyware company warned could put it out of business. In a 25-page ruling handed down Friday, U.S.
item.dc:creator: Raphael Satter, NewsNation
